site.title: VIVIYAN CORP LIMITED
site.description: VIVIYAN CORP LIMITED — Smart solutions for modern business.

about.descriptionLead: VIVIYAN CORP LIMITED helps businesses turn ideas into working solutions. We combine practical thinking, structured execution, and a clear business focus to
about.descriptionHighlight: support companies at different stages of growth.

services.label: Services
services.title: We provide the support businesses need to operate and grow better.
services.description: Business moves fast, and strong execution matters. VIVIYAN CORP LIMITED offers practical support and tailored solutions that help companies improve workflows, strengthen delivery, and work with more confidence.

values.sectionSubtitle: Our values shape how we work on daily basis

cards.title: Transparency
cards.description: We believe strong work starts with clear communication. No unnecessary complexity, no vague promises — just honest dialogue, realistic planning, and shared understanding at every stage.

cards.title: Progress
cards.description: We value movement, not noise. Step by step, we help businesses improve processes, strengthen operations, and move closer to their goals with confidence.

cards.title: Partnership
cards.description: We do not see cooperation as a formal process. We build long-term relationships based on trust, responsibility, and a real commitment to the people we work with.

footer.ctaText: Stay aligned with business that moves forward.
footer.copyright: © 2026 VIVIYAN CORP LIMITED. All rights reserved.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
intro: This Privacy Policy explains how VIVIYAN CORP LIMITED ("Company", "We", "Our", or "Us"), a company incorporated and registered in the Republic of Cyprus, collects, uses, and protects your personal data when you visit or interact with this website.
We are committed to processing personal data in accordance with applicable data protection laws, including the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679.
This Privacy Policy should be read together with our Terms and Conditions, which govern your use of this Website.

sections.title: Data Controller
content: For the purposes of applicable data protection laws, VIVIYAN CORP LIMITED acts as the data controller of your personal data.
If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or how your data is handled, you can contact us at the bottom "Get in touch" on the Website.

sections.title: Personal Data We Collect
introLines: We may collect and process different categories of personal data in connection with your use of this website.
This includes, in particular, personal data that you voluntarily provide to us, as well as certain information that is collected automatically when you access or interact with the website.
The categories of personal data we may collect include:
content: Contact information, such as your name, email address, phone number, or any other details you provide when contacting us or submitting an inquiry.
Communication data, including any messages, requests, or correspondence you send to us, and records of our communications with you.
Technical data, such as your IP address, browser type and version, device type, operating system, and other technology-related identifiers.
Usage data, including information about how you access, navigate, and interact with the website, such as pages viewed, time spent on pages, and referring URLs.
afterList: Some of this data is collected automatically through the use of cookies or similar technologies when you visit the website.
You are not required to provide personal data to use the Website; however, certain features (such as contacting the Company) may not be available without providing relevant information.

sections.title: How We Use Your Data
introLines: We process personal data for specific, legitimate purposes and only to the extent necessary in connection with the operation of this website and our business activities.
In particular, we may use your personal data for the following purposes:
content: To respond to your inquiries, requests, or communications and to maintain correspondence with you.
To operate, manage, and maintain the website, including ensuring its functionality, performance, and availability.
To improve and develop the website, including analyzing usage patterns and user interactions.
To ensure the security of the website, prevent unauthorized access, and detect, investigate, or prevent misuse, fraud, or other unlawful activities.
To comply with applicable legal, regulatory, or compliance obligations, including record-keeping and responding to lawful requests from authorities.
To establish, exercise, or defend legal claims, where necessary.
afterList: Where applicable, we may also use your data to manage and document our internal administrative processes, including maintaining records of communications and interactions.

We process personal data only where there is an appropriate legal basis under applicable data protection laws, and we do not use your personal data for purposes that are incompatible with those described above.
We do not use your personal data for automated decision-making or profiling that produces legal or similarly significant effects.

sections.title: Legal Basis for Processing
introLines: We process your personal data only where we have a valid legal basis under applicable data protection laws, including the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679.
Depending on the context in which your personal data is collected and used, we rely on one or more of the following legal grounds:
content: Consent – where you have given clear and explicit consent to the processing of your personal data for one or more specific purposes. Where processing is based on consent, you have the right to withdraw your consent at any time, without affecting the lawfulness of processing carried out prior to such withdrawal.

Legitimate interests – where processing is necessary for the purposes of the legitimate interests pursued by the Company, provided that such interests are not overridden by your fundamental rights and freedoms. These legitimate interests may include operating and improving the website, responding to inquiries, ensuring security, preventing misuse, and managing our business activities.
Legal obligation – where processing is necessary for compliance with a legal or regulatory obligation to which the Company is subject.
afterList: Where required under applicable law, we will ensure that an appropriate assessment is carried out to confirm that our legitimate interests are not overridden by your rights and freedoms.

We will only process your personal data for the purposes for which it was collected, unless we reasonably consider that we need to use it for another compatible purpose in accordance with applicable law.

sections.title: Data Sharing
introLines: We do not sell, rent, or otherwise disclose your personal data to third parties for their own marketing or commercial purposes.
However, we may share your personal data with selected third parties where necessary for the purposes described in this Privacy Policy. Such recipients may include:
content: Service providers and business partners who provide services on our behalf, such as website hosting, IT support, analytics, or other technical services.
Professional advisors, including legal, accounting, and consulting providers, where necessary for the operation of our business or compliance with legal obligations.
Public authorities, regulators, or law enforcement bodies, where disclosure is required by applicable law or necessary to protect our legal rights.
afterList: All third parties with whom we share personal data are required to process such data in accordance with applicable data protection laws and to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure its security and confidentiality.

Where personal data is transferred to or accessed from countries outside the European Economic Area (EEA), we ensure that such transfers are carried out in compliance with applicable data protection laws and that appropriate safeguards are in place.
Such safeguards may include, where applicable:
secondList: The use of Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the European Commission.
Transfers to jurisdictions that have been recognized by the European Commission as providing an adequate level of data protection.
Other legally recognized transfer mechanisms under applicable law.
afterSecondList: We take reasonable steps to ensure that any international transfers of personal data are adequately protected and that your rights under applicable data protection laws are preserved.

sections.title: Data Subject Rights
introLines: Under applicable data protection laws, including the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679, you have a number of rights in relation to your personal data.
In particular, you have the right to:
content: Access your personal data and obtain information about how it is processed.
Rectification, meaning you may request correction of inaccurate or incomplete personal data.
Erasure ("right to be forgotten"), allowing you to request deletion of your personal data where applicable.
Restriction of processing, where you may request that we limit the way we use your data under certain circumstances.
Object to processing, including where processing is based on legitimate interests.
Data portability, allowing you to receive your personal data in a structured, commonly used, and machine-readable format, and to transmit it to another controller where technically feasible.
Withdraw consent at any time, where processing is based on your consent, without affecting the lawfulness of processing carried out prior to withdrawal.
afterList: You may exercise your rights by contacting us using the contact details provided in this Privacy Policy.
We may request additional information to verify your identity before responding to your request.
We will respond to all valid requests in accordance with applicable data protection laws.

You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the competent supervisory authority in the Republic of Cyprus, in particular the Office of the Commissioner for Personal Data Protection, if you believe that your data has been processed unlawfully.

sections.title: Data Security and Retention
content: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures designed to protect personal data against unauthorized or unlawful processing, as well as against accidental loss, destruction, or damage.

Such measures may include, where appropriate, access controls, data minimization, secure storage systems, and other internal policies aimed at safeguarding personal data and ensuring its confidentiality and integrity.

Access to personal data is limited to those persons who have a legitimate need to access it in connection with the purposes described in this Privacy Policy and who are subject to appropriate confidentiality obligations.

We retain personal data only for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes for which it was collected, including for the purposes of satisfying any legal, regulatory, accounting, or reporting requirements.
When determining the appropriate retention period, we take into account the nature and sensitivity of the personal data, the purposes for which it is processed, and applicable legal obligations.
Once personal data is no longer required, we will take reasonable steps to securely delete, anonymize, or otherwise dispose of it.

While we take appropriate measures to protect personal data, no transmission over the internet or method of electronic storage can be guaranteed to be completely secure. Accordingly, we cannot guarantee absolute security.

sections.title: Data Breach
content: We take appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect personal data against unauthorized access, loss, misuse, or disclosure. However, despite these measures, no system can be guaranteed to be completely secure.
In the event of a personal data breach, we will assess the nature and impact of the incident and take appropriate steps to mitigate any risks to individuals.

Where required under applicable data protection laws, including the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679, we will: notify the competent supervisory authority within the required timeframe; inform affected individuals without undue delay where the breach is likely to result in a high risk to their rights and freedoms.
We will also take reasonable steps to investigate the breach, prevent further incidents, and implement corrective measures as necessary.

sections.title: Cookies
content: This website may use cookies and similar technologies to enhance user experience, ensure the proper functioning of the website, and analyze how the website is used.

Cookies are small text files that are stored on your device when you visit a website. They may be used to recognize your device, remember preferences, and collect information about your interaction with the website.

We may use different types of cookies, including those necessary for the operation of the website, as well as analytical or performance-related cookies that help us understand how users interact with the website.
You can control or disable cookies through your browser settings. Please note that disabling certain cookies may affect the functionality and performance of the website.
We will request your consent before placing non-essential cookies on your device.

sections.title: Final Provisions
content: This Privacy Policy is effective as of the date indicated at the top of this document.

We reserve the right to amend, update, or revise this Privacy Policy at any time, at our sole discretion. Any changes will become effective upon publication on this website, unless otherwise stated. The "Last updated" date will be revised accordingly.
We encourage you to review this Privacy Policy periodically to stay informed about how we process and protect your personal data.
This Privacy Policy shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Cyprus.
Nothing in this Privacy Policy shall limit or exclude any rights you may have under applicable data protection laws, including the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679.

If any provision of this Privacy Policy is found to be invalid or unenforceable under applicable law, such provision shall be deemed modified to the extent necessary to make it valid and enforceable, and the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.
For any questions regarding this Privacy Policy or the processing of your personal data, you may contact us.

terms.title: Terms and Conditions
intro: Welcome to the website of VIVIYAN CORP LIMITED ("Company", "We", "Our", or "Us"), a company incorporated and registered in the Republic of Cyprus.

These Terms and Conditions ("Terms") constitute a legally binding agreement between you, as a visitor or user ("You", "Your", "Visitor") of this website https://viviyan.co ("Website"), and the Company. By accessing or using this website, you agree to be bound by this agreement. If you do not agree, please do not use the Website.
Your use of the Website is also governed by our Privacy Policy, which describes how we collect, use, and protect personal data.
The Company provides general business-related information and may offer various services from time to time. However, no specific services are described or guaranteed on this website.

sections.title: Use of the Website
introLines: This Website is made available for informational purposes and to enable users to learn about the Company and contact it.
You may use this Website to:
content: Access and view information published by the Company.
Learn about the Company and its activities.
Contact the Company or submit inquiries.
Use any available features of the website as intended.
afterList: You agree to use this Website only for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms. You are responsible for ensuring that your use of the website complies with all applicable laws and regulations.
You must not use this website in any way that:
secondList: Violates any applicable laws or regulations.
Infringes the rights of the Company or any third party.
Is unlawful, fraudulent, misleading, or harmful.
Interferes with the operation, security, or availability of the website.
Restricts or disrupts the use of the website by others.
afterSecondList: In particular, you agree not to:
thirdList: Attempt to gain unauthorized access to the website or related systems.
Introduce viruses, malware, or other harmful code.
Use automated tools (such as bots or scrapers) without authorization.
Copy, reproduce, or exploit any content without permission.
Engage in any activity that could damage or impair the website.
afterThirdList: The Company reserves the right, at its sole discretion and without prior notice, to restrict, suspend, or terminate access to the website, or to take appropriate legal action in case of violations.

sections.title: Services
sections.intro: All services that may be offered by the Company:
content: Are subject to separate agreements, contracts, or written arrangements between the Company and the client.
May be modified, suspended, or discontinued at any time without prior notice.
Are provided subject to availability, internal capacity, and the Company's discretion.
May be governed by additional terms and conditions specific to each engagement.
afterList: Nothing on this Website constitutes or shall be deemed to constitute legally binding offer, commitment to provide any services, or a guarantee of results, outcomes, or performance.
Any information presented on this website is for general informational purposes only and does not constitute professional, legal, financial, or business advice.
The Company reserves the right to refuse service to any person or entity at its sole discretion, to the extent permitted by applicable law.

sections.title: Intellectual Property
content: All content available on this website, including, without limitation, all text, graphics, logos, design elements, layout, visual materials, and any other content, is the property of Company or its licensors and is protected by applicable intellectual property laws.

You may access and use the content of this website solely for your personal or internal business purposes. Any other use of the website content, including copying, reproduction, modification, distribution, publication, display, or commercial exploitation, in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of the Company, is strictly prohibited.

Nothing in these Terms and Conditions shall be construed as granting, by implication or otherwise, any license or right to use any intellectual property of the Company without its prior written consent.
The Company reserves all rights in and to its intellectual property. Unauthorized use of any materials on this website may result in legal action.

sections.title: Limitation of Liability
content: This Website and all content made available through it are provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis.

To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, the Company makes no representations or warranties of any kind, whether express or implied, regarding the website or its content. In particular, the Company does not warrant that the website will be uninterrupted, secure, or free from errors or defects, or that any information provided is accurate, complete, reliable, or up to date.

The content of this Website is provided for general informational purposes only and should not be relied upon as professional, legal, financial, or business advice. Any reliance you place on such information is strictly at your own risk.

To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, Company shall not be liable for any loss or damage arising out of or in connection with your use of, or inability to use, this website or its content. This includes, without limitation, any indirect, incidental, consequential, or special damages, as well as any loss of business, profits, revenue, data, or goodwill, even if the Company has been advised of the possibility of such damages.

sections.title: Third-Party Links
content: This Website may contain links to third-party websites or resources that are not owned or controlled by the Company. Such links are provided for convenience and informational purposes only.

The Company has no control over, and assumes no responsibility for, the content, availability, accuracy, or practices of any third-party websites. The inclusion of any links does not imply any endorsement, approval, or association with such third parties.
Accessing third-party websites is done at your own risk. You are encouraged to review the terms and conditions and privacy policies of any third-party websites You visit.

sections.title: Changes to Terms
content: The Company reserves the right to modify, update, or revise these Terms at any time, at its sole discretion.
Any changes will become effective immediately upon being published on this website, unless otherwise stated. The "Last updated" date at the top of this page will be revised accordingly.

By continuing to access or use the Website after any such changes, you agree to be bound by the updated Terms and Conditions. If you do not agree with the revised terms, you should discontinue use of the website.

sections.title: Governing Law
content: These Terms and Conditions, and any dispute or claim arising out of or in connection with them or their subject matter, shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Cyprus.
Any disputes arising out of or in connection with these Terms shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Republic of Cyprus.

sections.title: Contact Information
content: If you have any questions, concerns, or requests regarding these Terms and Conditions, you may contact the Company using the bottom "Get in touch" on the Website.
